title: Active Transport and Exchange Surfaces
description: Understand active transport and why exchange surfaces are adapted.
learningOutcomes: Compare active transport with diffusion and osmosis.
Explain key adaptations of exchange surfaces.

outcomes.text: Explain why active transport needs energy.

terms.definition: Movement of substances against a concentration gradient using energy from respiration.
A surface where substances move between two environments.
Finger-like projections in the small intestine that increase surface area.

content.title: When diffusion is not enough
content.content: Diffusion only moves particles from high to low concentration. Active transport lets cells absorb substances from low to high concentration, so it requires energy from respiration.

questions.questionText: Why does active transport require energy?
expectedAnswer: it moves against the concentration gradient
because substances move from low to high concentration

questions.questionText: Name one adaptation of an exchange surface.
Compare diffusion and active transport in one sentence.
expectedAnswer: diffusion is down a gradient while active transport is against a gradient using energy
active transport needs energy but diffusion does not
